data.title: Sectra invites investors to presentation of 9-month report for 2025/2026 with President and CFO in Sweden on Feb. 20, 2026.
data.description: A Swedish company specializing in medical imaging IT and cybersecurity, Sectra (STO: SECT B), based in Linköping, has announced its invitation to investors, analysts, and media for a presentation regarding its nine-month report. The presentation will feature company President Torbjörn Kronander and CFO Jessica Holmquist.

data.title: US stock futures up despite Thursday's drop, awaiting Q4 GDP data release. 10-year Treasury bond yields 4.07%. Indices up: Dow Jones 0.26%, S&P 500 0.37%, Nasdaq 100 0.47%, Russell 2000 0.34%. SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF also rose in premarket. Dropbox fell 4.16%, Copart dropped 6.64% post earnings reports. Fed likely to keep rates unchanged.
data.description: U.S. stock futures were up on Friday following a dip on Thursday, with all major benchmark indices showing positive trends. Investors await the fourth-quarter GDP data and the Personal Consumption Expenditure Price Index release. President Trump's ultimatum to Iran regarding its nuclear deal saw Tehran threatening retaliation against U.S. bases. The 10-year Treasury bond yield was at 4.07%, and the two-year bond at 3.47%. The FedWatch tool predicts a 94% chance of unchanged interest rates in March. Stock indices performed: Dow Jones +0.26%, S&P 500 +0.37%, Nasdaq 100 +0.47%, Russell 2000 +0.34%. SPY and QQQ ETFs were up premarket. Dropbox fell 4.16% despite strong Q4 earnings, predicting FY2026 sales slightly below estimates. Copart dropped 6.64% after disappointing Q2 earnings. Full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: CSG group's Excalibur Intl. to supply tens of thousands of artillery/mortar ammo to NATO country in Western Europe in low hundreds of millions euros.
data.description: Excalibur International, a company under the CSG group, has secured a deal with the Ministry of Defense of a NATO member country in Western Europe. The contract entails the provision of tens of thousands of artillery and mortar ammunition, amounting to a total value in the low hundreds of millions of euros. The agreement was granted through a fast-tracked tender process, responding to the critical requirement of restocking the ammunition inventory for the armed forces of the NATO member country.

data.title: Metaplanet CEO denies allegations of concealing Bitcoin trades and losses amid growing investor backlash.
data.description: Simon Gerovich, from Metaplanet, responded to allegations of concealing losses and important information regarding their Bitcoin investments amidst growing investor discontent over leveraged Bitcoin holdings. The company has come under fire for not disclosing key details, with the backlash intensifying due to the increasing number of leveraged Bitcoin treasuries.

data.title: Critics slam South Korea for $43B Bithumb Bitcoin mistake as 620,000 BTC mistakenly credited, probe hindered.
data.description: Lawmakers are expressing criticism towards South Korea's Financial Services Commission in the wake of a mistake by Bithumb, where 620,000 BTC was mistakenly credited. The probe into this incident is encountering delays.
